trivia-101.opentdb.bd30c1875934370b.explanation: Astronauts made six crewed landings on the Moon during NASA's Apollo program.
trivia-101.opentdb.bd30c1875934370b.details: Apollo 11 made the first landing in 1969 and Apollo 17 made the last in 1972. Apollo 13 flew toward the Moon but did not land after an onboard explosion.

trivia-101.opentdb.09317bf1d975f72b.explanation: Stargate Universe ran for two seasons.
trivia-101.opentdb.09317bf1d975f72b.details: The series followed a crew stranded aboard the ancient starship Destiny. Syfy aired 40 episodes between 2009 and 2011.

trivia-101.opentdb.7969e1066cb1ca9b.explanation: Adiós is a standard Spanish word for goodbye.
trivia-101.opentdb.7969e1066cb1ca9b.details: It can carry a more final tone than hasta luego, which literally means “until later.” The accent falls on the final syllable.

trivia-101.opentdb.4134b68f7b005189.explanation: Typical human body cells carry two copies of most genes, one inherited from each parent.
trivia-101.opentdb.4134b68f7b005189.details: Humans are diploid: most cells have 23 pairs of chromosomes. Important exceptions include mature red blood cells, which lack nuclei, and eggs and sperm, which carry one chromosome set.

trivia-101.opentdb.43ade97d762d36e4.explanation: In England, the gridiron sport is normally called American football.
trivia-101.opentdb.43ade97d762d36e4.details: The word football by itself usually refers to association football—the sport Americans call soccer. The added adjective prevents confusion between the two games.

trivia-101.opentdb.8f45b3c242b2ff09.explanation: Voyager establishes real-time contact with Starfleet in the episode Pathfinder.
trivia-101.opentdb.8f45b3c242b2ff09.details: The sixth-season episode centers on Reginald Barclay's efforts to communicate with the stranded ship through an experimental communications project.

trivia-101.opentdb.801c54fddc854c3f.explanation: The Jamocha Shake is an Arby's menu item.
trivia-101.opentdb.801c54fddc854c3f.details: “Jamocha” blends the ideas of java and mocha: the shake combines coffee and chocolate flavors rather than tasting like a plain vanilla shake.

trivia-101.opentdb.1630b6594ee7c4b0.explanation: Henry VIII was the second monarch of the House of Tudor.
trivia-101.opentdb.1630b6594ee7c4b0.details: His father, Henry VII, founded the Tudor dynasty after winning the Battle of Bosworth Field. Henry VIII was followed by his children Edward VI, Mary I, and Elizabeth I.

trivia-101.opentdb.f6db4ba256b6052f.explanation: The Chevrolet LS1 V8 displaces 346 cubic inches, or about 5.7 litres.
trivia-101.opentdb.f6db4ba256b6052f.details: General Motors introduced the LS1 in the 1997 Corvette. Its compact pushrod design became the foundation of the widely used LS engine family.

trivia-101.opentdb.c2cfbc2d306cf7cc.explanation: “Nobody puts Baby in a corner” is the signature line from Dirty Dancing.
trivia-101.opentdb.c2cfbc2d306cf7cc.details: Patrick Swayze's Johnny says it before bringing Frances “Baby” Houseman onto the dance floor for the film's climactic performance.

trivia-101.opentdb.6e0fd4df19de972a.explanation: Cynophobia is an intense fear of dogs.
trivia-101.opentdb.6e0fd4df19de972a.details: The name combines Greek roots: kyōn means dog and phobos means fear. A phobia is stronger and more persistent than ordinary caution.

trivia-101.opentdb.1eba43c67ec5eeca.explanation: Carbon-12 has an atomic mass of exactly 12 atomic mass units and is the usual reference answer.
trivia-101.opentdb.1eba43c67ec5eeca.details: The periodic table's average atomic weight for natural carbon is about 12.011 because it includes small amounts of heavier isotopes, especially carbon-13.

trivia-101.opentdb.0e1712ef9f61604d.explanation: Washington, D.C., is the capital of the United States.
trivia-101.opentdb.0e1712ef9f61604d.details: The federal district is not part of any state. It was established along the Potomac River and named for George Washington.

trivia-101.opentdb.c69c4eee8d3838a3.explanation: The Koenigsegg Agera RS set a widely recognized production-car speed record in 2017.
trivia-101.opentdb.c69c4eee8d3838a3.details: Its two-way average was about 277.9 mph on a closed Nevada highway. “Fastest” records depend on date and testing rules, so this fact should be understood as a historical record rather than a permanent current title.

trivia-101.opentdb.283844ee4d6c3eac.explanation: The French word for hat is chapeau.
trivia-101.opentdb.283844ee4d6c3eac.details: The plural is chapeaux. English also borrows the expression chapeau! as a figurative tip of the hat meaning “well done.”

trivia-101.opentdb.8f56ce62163b546e.explanation: Electrical resistance is measured in ohms.
trivia-101.opentdb.8f56ce62163b546e.details: The unit's symbol is Ω, the Greek capital omega. It is named for physicist Georg Ohm, whose law relates voltage, current, and resistance.

trivia-101.opentdb.b222980de7281699.explanation: Traditional marzipan is made primarily from ground almonds and sugar.
trivia-101.opentdb.b222980de7281699.details: The confection can be shaped into candies, used to cover cakes, or rolled into decorative figures. Apricot-kernel products are similar but are usually called persipan.

trivia-101.opentdb.2aa2bf4e5b71d505.explanation: Rogue One takes place immediately before Episode IV, after the events of Episode III.
trivia-101.opentdb.2aa2bf4e5b71d505.details: The film follows the mission to steal the Death Star plans. Its final moments lead directly into the opening pursuit in A New Hope.

trivia-101.opentdb.7a5aaa8e5d0de34b.explanation: Eddie the Mean Old Yeti serves as Donkey Kong's guardian angel in It's a Wonderful Life.
trivia-101.opentdb.7a5aaa8e5d0de34b.details: The episode borrows its title and alternate-life premise from the classic film It's a Wonderful Life, recasting the idea with characters from Donkey Kong Country.

trivia-101.opentdb.9ebdcc4178369ac6.explanation: The “Mac” in Fleetwood Mac comes from bassist John McVie.
trivia-101.opentdb.9ebdcc4178369ac6.details: Drummer Mick Fleetwood supplies the first half of the name. Founder Peter Green combined his rhythm section's surnames when naming the band.

trivia-101.opentdb.d9a9d54b9c3df34c.explanation: Goodyear makes the Eagle line of performance tires and supplies NASCAR's national series.
trivia-101.opentdb.d9a9d54b9c3df34c.details: The company is named for Charles Goodyear, associated with the vulcanization of rubber, although he did not found the tire company.

trivia-101.opentdb.22802193c3800f34.explanation: Brazil has the largest population of Japanese descent outside Japan.
trivia-101.opentdb.22802193c3800f34.details: Large-scale Japanese immigration to Brazil began in 1908. São Paulo and Paraná became major centers of the Japanese Brazilian community.

trivia-101.opentdb.0a202d74e3632689.explanation: “Let them eat cake” is traditionally attributed to Marie Antoinette, but there is no good evidence she said it.
trivia-101.opentdb.0a202d74e3632689.details: A similar line appeared in Jean-Jacques Rousseau's writing before Marie Antoinette became queen. The attribution survived because it symbolized an elite supposedly oblivious to ordinary hunger.

trivia-101.opentdb.74c966721bafea89.explanation: Nitrogen makes up about 78 percent of Earth's dry atmosphere.
trivia-101.opentdb.74c966721bafea89.details: Oxygen accounts for roughly 21 percent, with argon, carbon dioxide, and trace gases making up most of the remainder.

trivia-101.opentdb.15eaa9c8c1da2a7d.explanation: Fiat was founded in Turin, Italy.
trivia-101.opentdb.15eaa9c8c1da2a7d.details: The name began as an acronym for Fabbrica Italiana Automobili Torino—Italian Automobile Factory of Turin.

trivia-101.opentdb.dd5c02e8f04be361.explanation: Dave Grohl was Nirvana's drummer before founding Foo Fighters.
trivia-101.opentdb.dd5c02e8f04be361.details: After Kurt Cobain's death ended Nirvana, Grohl recorded the first Foo Fighters album largely by himself before assembling a band.

trivia-101.opentdb.70d41876eb243f77.explanation: Tony Montana says “Say hello to my little friend!” in Scarface.
trivia-101.opentdb.70d41876eb243f77.details: Al Pacino delivers the line during the 1983 film's climactic shootout, referring to the grenade launcher attached to his rifle.

trivia-101.opentdb.a81f4cc798ec8a0d.explanation: Ben Shapiro is the non-NPR host among the listed choices.
trivia-101.opentdb.a81f4cc798ec8a0d.details: Shapiro is associated with the conservative media outlet The Daily Wire and hosts The Ben Shapiro Show, rather than an NPR program.

trivia-101.opentdb.8c052dedd910b6b7.explanation: Honshu is the largest of Japan's islands.
trivia-101.opentdb.8c052dedd910b6b7.details: It contains Tokyo, Kyoto, Osaka, and Mount Fuji. Hokkaido, Kyushu, and Shikoku are the other three principal islands.

trivia-101.opentdb.2ee4e666a193ee37.explanation: Clint Eastwood was not one of the seven gunfighters in the 1960 film The Magnificent Seven.
trivia-101.opentdb.2ee4e666a193ee37.details: The ensemble included Yul Brynner, Steve McQueen, Charles Bronson, Robert Vaughn, Brad Dexter, James Coburn, and Horst Buchholz.

trivia-101.opentdb.0f441861b7ecbcfd.explanation: Stitch! is a Japanese anime spin-off of Disney's Lilo & Stitch franchise.
trivia-101.opentdb.0f441861b7ecbcfd.details: Unlike the original Hawaii-set story, the series places Stitch in Japan and pairs him with a girl named Yuna.

trivia-101.opentdb.7b544deb2dd1dd35.explanation: Alberta is a province of Canada, not an Australian state or territory.
trivia-101.opentdb.7b544deb2dd1dd35.details: Australia has six states and several territories. Alberta lies in western Canada between British Columbia and Saskatchewan.

trivia-101.opentdb.3472de3135faed88.explanation: Silver was not one of the five Allied landing beaches on D-Day.
trivia-101.opentdb.3472de3135faed88.details: The Normandy beach code names were Utah, Omaha, Gold, Juno, and Sword. American forces landed at Utah and Omaha, British forces at Gold and Sword, and Canadians at Juno.

trivia-101.opentdb.899d5f90d3680e4f.explanation: Wayne Gretzky holds the NHL career record with 2,857 regular-season points.
trivia-101.opentdb.899d5f90d3680e4f.details: His total consists of 894 goals and 1,963 assists. Gretzky remains the career points leader even if all of his goals are removed.

trivia-101.opentdb.e801fd1bd973c275.explanation: Californication, Can't Stop, and Under the Bridge are songs by the Red Hot Chili Peppers.
trivia-101.opentdb.e801fd1bd973c275.details: The Los Angeles band blends rock, funk, and punk influences. Under the Bridge became one of its defining early-1990s hits.

trivia-101.opentdb.9780c32f7aa359de.explanation: The Montreal Canadiens have won more Stanley Cups than any other NHL team.
trivia-101.opentdb.9780c32f7aa359de.details: The franchise has 24 championships and is one of the NHL's Original Six teams. Canadiens is the club's proper French-derived spelling.

trivia-101.opentdb.97442a76c96d05da.explanation: Roger Federer is frequently called “the Maestro” for his graceful and precise style of play.
trivia-101.opentdb.97442a76c96d05da.details: The nickname emphasizes Federer's fluid technique, court craft, and apparent ease rather than referring to an official tennis title.

trivia-101.opentdb.0006279f0d344f5d.prompt: Who is the author of Jurassic Park?
trivia-101.opentdb.0006279f0d344f5d.explanation: Michael Crichton wrote the novel Jurassic Park.
trivia-101.opentdb.0006279f0d344f5d.details: Published in 1990, the techno-thriller explores genetic engineering and chaos theory. Steven Spielberg directed the successful 1993 film adaptation.

trivia-101.opentdb.70418dfa69a82068.explanation: Chris Martin is Coldplay's lead singer and primary frontman.
trivia-101.opentdb.70418dfa69a82068.details: He also plays piano and guitar and shares songwriting duties with bandmates Jonny Buckland, Guy Berryman, and Will Champion.

trivia-101.opentdb.f7c47e7cd9cf0725.explanation: Real Madrid won the 2017 UEFA Champions League final.
trivia-101.opentdb.f7c47e7cd9cf0725.details: Madrid defeated Juventus 4–1 in Cardiff and became the first club in the Champions League era to retain the trophy in consecutive seasons.

trivia-101.opentdb.d9df6ac5e24300d6.explanation: Formula One introduced the Virtual Safety Car after Jules Bianchi's fatal 2014 Japanese Grand Prix crash.
trivia-101.opentdb.d9df6ac5e24300d6.details: The system slows every car to a controlled delta time without deploying the physical safety car, reducing speed consistently around a hazardous track.

trivia-101.opentdb.5fa652b7e134c0e6.explanation: McDonald's dates its founding to 1955, when Ray Kroc opened his first franchised restaurant.
trivia-101.opentdb.5fa652b7e134c0e6.details: Richard and Maurice McDonald had opened their original restaurant earlier, but 1955 marks the beginning of Kroc's corporation and global franchise expansion.

trivia-101.opentdb.83bde02035017c23.explanation: Sn is the chemical symbol for tin.
trivia-101.opentdb.83bde02035017c23.details: The symbol comes from stannum, the Latin word for tin. Tin has atomic number 50 and is used in alloys such as bronze and solder.

trivia-101.opentdb.a48ce10eba12bc0f.explanation: The World Health Organization is headquartered in Geneva, Switzerland.
trivia-101.opentdb.a48ce10eba12bc0f.details: WHO is the United Nations agency responsible for international public health. It was established in 1948.

trivia-101.opentdb.c37ee4350a39f8a9.explanation: Tony Soprano becomes emotionally attached to the racehorse Pie-O-My.
trivia-101.opentdb.c37ee4350a39f8a9.details: The horse appears during the fourth season of The Sopranos and exposes a tender side of Tony that contrasts with his treatment of people.

trivia-101.opentdb.ca7652453dec9850.explanation: Australia became a federation on January 1, 1901.
trivia-101.opentdb.ca7652453dec9850.details: The six self-governing British colonies united as states of the Commonwealth of Australia under a new federal constitution.

trivia-101.opentdb.d25e0db829aeca17.explanation: Honda is the world's largest motorcycle manufacturer by production volume.
trivia-101.opentdb.d25e0db829aeca17.details: The Japanese company began producing motorized bicycles after World War II and grew into a major maker of motorcycles, automobiles, and engines.

trivia-101.opentdb.4a61dd51ff287328.explanation: The Niagara Mohawk Building in Syracuse, New York, is a prominent Art Deco structure.
trivia-101.opentdb.4a61dd51ff287328.details: Completed in 1932 for an electric utility, its geometric ornament, stepped profile, and dramatic lighting celebrate electricity and modern industry.

trivia-101.opentdb.e80020046f17987b.explanation: Comedian Gilbert Gottfried voiced Iago in Disney's 1992 Aladdin.
trivia-101.opentdb.e80020046f17987b.details: Iago is Jafar's loud, sarcastic parrot sidekick. Gottfried's distinctive raspy delivery became central to the character.

trivia-101.opentdb.cd6d08d200040d39.explanation: Matt Bellamy is the lead singer, guitarist, and principal songwriter of Muse.
trivia-101.opentdb.cd6d08d200040d39.details: His dramatic vocals, guitar work, and piano playing help define the British band's blend of alternative rock, electronics, and classical influence.
